methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $380M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia is a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised $380M+ to date. The relevant comp universe comprises 7 private frontier-tech peers in transportation, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Slate Auto, and Harbinger; no public comparables exist in this subsector.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method here. Using total raised ($380M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-later-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $646M. A peer-median approach was not viable: while 3 transportation peers exist, none met the threshold of ≥3 same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers required for reliable cross-sectional benchmarking.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $549M to $743M, with a median of $646M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the stability of the 1.7x multiplier for this particular subsector and technology maturity profile—transportation hardware ventures may face different step-up dynamics than software-forward peers. The thin comp set and absence of public market reference points further limit confidence intervals. Additional near-term raises or material operational milestones would warrant re-anchoring this analysis.
